prompt: Create a TalentOps Compliance & Service Desk platform that runs as an enterprise-grade offline desktop application for HR operations teams to manage recruiting projects, service catalog pricing, and internal approvals in a single workstation workflow. Primary roles include Administrator (system setup, templates, permissions), Recruiter/Coordinator (job postings, candidate intake, resume QA), Reviewer/Compliance Officer (violation review and audit), and Approver (multi-level credit change sign-off). The desktop UX must be keyboard-first and multi-window: users can keep a Recruiting Project window, Candidate Detail window, and Approval Inbox open in parallel, with right-click context menus for “Tag candidate,” “Request missing materials,” and “Create approval task.” Provide global shortcuts (e.g., Ctrl+K quick search, Ctrl+Enter save, Alt+N next record), deep clipboard integration for copying structured fields, and a system-tray presence that can badge pending approvals and overdue tasks without relying on push services. Detail pages must use componentized rendering with configurable blocks (basic info, high-resolution images, stamp/verification notes, related works, cited sources) and consistent placeholders: missing required fields show “Required” and prevent status changes until resolved. All text fields must support English plus one additional locale (e.g., Spanish) with localized dates (MM/DD/YYYY) and currency formatting ($). Recruiting supports batch hiring projects, configurable resume templates and field rules, structured resumes with version history (each save creates a new version, max 50 versions retained), attachment import (PDF/DOCX) with basic metadata extraction (file name, size, page count when available) and quality checks (required sections present, maximum 10 MB per attachment, allowed extensions only). Violation detection must be rule-based and local (e.g., prohibited phrases list, missing EEOC disposition, duplicate SSN pattern flags) and routed into a review queue with decision, comments, and an immutable audit trail. The application must be built with Electron for cross-platform desktop delivery on Windows 11, using Angular for the UI, Fastify for the local application server layer, and PostgreSQL running locally (bundled installer) for persistence; installer delivered as a signed .msi with an offline update mechanism that imports a versioned update package from disk/USB and supports one-click rollback to the previous version. Data models include RecruitingProject, JobPosting, Candidate, ResumeVersion, Attachment, Tag, ServiceCategory/Attribute/Specification, PricingRule, CapacityPlan, ApprovalRequest, ApprovalStep, Comment, and NotificationTask. Service Catalog supports category/tag/attribute management and configurable specs (duration in 15-minute increments, headcount 1–20, tools/add-ons list capped at 30 items), pricing rules (base price, tiered pricing thresholds, surcharges such as “after-hours +$25.00”), listing status (Draft/Active/Paused/Retired), and capacity controls (daily order-taking volume per service with hard stop once reached). Credit Change workflow must support multi-level approvals with joint-sign (all must approve) or any-sign (first approval completes), required comments for rejection, optional attachments up to 20 MB, and final write-back only after the last approval step is completed; partial approvals remain pending and are visible in the audit log. Notifications and task reminders must be fully local: in-app inbox plus optional locally generated emails/SMS as exportable files (no sending), with template variables, retries for rendering failures, and delivery receipts recorded as “generated/opened/acknowledged.” Geospatial analytics and high-volume map rendering must be implemented as offline visualization of imported datasets (CSV/GeoJSON via file system, including data from GPS devices via USB): support administrative-region aggregation, grid/buffer analysis, POI density, and route/trajectory display with local spatial indexing and tile/layer rendering optimized to keep startup under 10 seconds and steady-state memory under 600 MB; long-running stability requires explicit resource disposal for media/map layers. For VOD multi-bitrate playback, support local HLS/DASH assets stored on disk with quality switching, 0.5x–2.0x speed, subtitles (SRT/VTT), picture-in-picture, and resume playback persisted locally; expose observable error codes (e.g., MEDIA_NOT_FOUND, SEGMENT_DECODE_FAIL) with automatic retries (3 attempts with 2/5/10 second backoff) and crash recovery via periodic checkpoints every 30 seconds to restore the last viewed record, draft forms, and the approval inbox state after restart. Sensitive fields (SSN, DOB, compensation) must be encrypted at rest and masked in UI by default, with role-based reveal requiring re-entry of the local password.
